subject: [PATCH v2 13/14] fuse: add zero-copy over io-uring

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch adds support for zero-copy data transfer over io-uring in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module, eliminating unnecessary memory copies between userspace, the kernel, and the fuse server for page-backed read/write operations. The feature requires CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges and buffer rings with pinned headers and buffers. When enabled, the kernel registers client pages as a sparse buffer at the entry's fixed id via io_buffer_register_bvec(), allowing the fuse server to perform direct io-uring read/write operations on these pages. Non-page-backed arguments still go through the payload buffer as normal. The patch also includes details on the request flow for zero-copy write operations.
